text:
There was a young boy named Oliver who loved owls. Not just any owls. He loved owls that could talk. Oliver had an old book that belonged to his grandfather that contained stories about owls and their habits. One particular owl in the book spoke an Owlanelogy that went something like this...

"Listen Owl-eye,
I dwell high up
In a branch above the bay...
It's pretty here,
You see those trees?
That's where I perch..."

Oliver imagined Owl-Eye flying around his own little tree owl sanctuary.

One chilly autumn afternoon, Oliver decided to sneak downstairs. His big brown eyes couldn't wait.

After careful maneuvering under the big goose feather softness?

A Hawkssey eyes?

Right!

He opened the book carefully.

And Owl-Eye spoke:

"...Oh young owlling,
What wonders you behold?
You think you know
The forest cold?

But you've never heard
Of Owlish delight?

You'll see something new.
Your feather
